message:
feat(alloc, btree, extent, inode, ondisk): add persist-through allocation and fix on-disk parsing

ffs-alloc: add start parameter to bitmap_find_contiguous() enabling
rotor-style allocation that avoids always scanning from bit 0. Add
alloc_inode_persist() and free_inode_persist() wrappers that write
updated group descriptors back to disk after in-memory bitmap changes,
ensuring on-disk state stays consistent for crash recovery.

ffs-btree: use wrapping_add(1) instead of saturating_add(1) for BW-tree
epoch advancement so the epoch counter wraps around at u64::MAX rather
than getting stuck, preventing a liveness issue after 2^64 CAS operations.

ffs-extent: thread PersistCtx through all allocation and free operations
(allocate_extent, allocate_unwritten_extent, truncate_extents, punch_hole,
mark_written) so block allocation changes are persisted to disk immediately.
Simplify truncate_extents() from a two-pass walk+iterate approach to a
single delete_range() call covering [new_logical_end, u32::MAX), which
is both simpler and avoids the repeated tree-walk overhead.

ffs-inode: thread PersistCtx through create_inode() and delete_inode()
so inode bitmap allocations and extent truncations write through to disk.
Fix encode_extra_timestamp() to shift nanoseconds left by 2 bits (nsec << 2)
instead of masking (nsec & 0x3FFF_FFFC), matching the ext4 on-disk format
where epoch occupies bits 0-1 and nanoseconds occupy bits 2-31.

ffs-ondisk: fix Ext4Inode size_hi parsing to only read the high 32 bits
for regular files (S_IFREG), matching the Linux kernel which uses the
i_dir_acl alias for directories. Fix dx_root/dx_entries parsing to read
count from the dx_countlimit structure at the correct offset and skip the
countlimit entry itself when building the entry list, aligning with the
kernel's htree layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
